title: Offline/PWA mode for low-connectivity users
context: Low-connectivity users — including rural communities, low-income households, and users in areas with poor network coverage — are among the most likely to need water-quality information and least likely to have fast or reliable internet. A Progressive Web App (PWA) offline mode reuses the existing static build artifacts and service worker caching to enable offline access. No new tracking, telemetry, or data collection must be introduced; the privacy posture must be maintained or improved.
objective: Add PWA offline mode to the static web app: (1) service worker that caches static artifacts (HTML, CSS, JS, pre-built explainers) for offline use; (2) reuses existing static build artifacts — no new data collection or tracking; (3) clear user indication when viewing cached/offline content with staleness information; (4) no tracking, telemetry, or PII collection introduced; (5) WCAG 2.2 AA maintained; (6) runtime egress E2E passes (no unexpected outbound requests in offline mode).
acceptanceCriteria: PWA service worker caches static artifacts for offline use; previously viewed explainers accessible without network
Offline mode reuses existing static build artifacts; no new data collection, telemetry, or tracking introduced
UI clearly indicates when content is served from cache (offline) and shows staleness/snapshot date
Runtime network-interception E2E passes in offline mode (no unexpected outbound requests)
WCAG 2.2 AA maintained; zero critical axe violations
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: PR adding PWA service worker and offline mode to the static web app.
